perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will hold the Confidential Information in confidence and will not disclose the Confidential Information to any person or entity without the prior written consent of the Owner.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient will hold the Confidential Information in confidence and may disclose the Confidential Information to its legal counsel without the prior written consent of the Owner.
perturbation.explanation: The altered section introduces an in-text contradiction by permitting the Recipient to disclose confidential information to their legal counsel without the Owner's consent, while the original clause explicitly prohibits any disclosure without prior written consent. This creates a direct contradiction within section II ('PROTECTION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION').
perturbation.contradicted_text: PROTECTION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORM ATION. The Recipient understands and acknowledges that the Confidential Information has been developed or obtained by the Owner by the investment of significant time, effort and expense, and that the Confidential Information is a valuable, special and unique asset of the Owner which provides the Owner with a significant competitive advantage, and needs to be protected from improper disclosure. In consideration for the receipt by the Recipient of the Confidential Information, the Recipient agrees as follows: A. No Disclosure. The Recipient will hold the Confidential Information in confidence and will not disclose the Confidential Information to any person or entity without the prior written consent of the Owner.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly allows disclosure of Confidential Information to legal counsel without the Owner's consent, directly contradicting the original clause ('A. No Disclosure') in the Contradicted Text, which prohibits any disclosure without prior written consent. The Perturbation Explanation correctly identifies this as creating a direct contradiction within section II, aligning with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall promptly advise the Owner if the Recipient becomes aware of any possible unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient shall advise the Owner within 30 days if the Recipient becomes aware of any possible unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'promptly advise' to 'advise the Owner within 30 days', the text introduces a contradiction, giving the recipient a specific timeframe to report potential breaches of confidentiality, while the original text requires immediate action. This contradiction is created within section II ('PROTECTION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION').
perturbation.contradicted_text: PROTECTION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORM ATION. The Recipient understands and acknowledges that the Confidential Information has been developed or obtained by the Owner by the investment of significant time, effort and expense, and that the Confidential Information is a valuable, special and unique asset of the Owner which provides the Owner with a significant competitive advantage, and needs to be protected from improper disclosure. In consideration for the receipt by the Recipient of the Confidential Information, the Recipient agrees as follows: A. No Disclosure. The Recipient will hold the Confidential Information in confidence and will not disclose the Confidential Information to any person or entity without the prior written consent of the Owner. B. No Copying/Modifying. The Recipient will not copy or modify any Confidential Information without the prior written consent of the Owner. C. Unauthorized Use. The Recipient shall promptly advise the Owner if the Recipient becomes aware of any possible unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Recipient must "promptly advise" the Owner, implying immediate action regarding unauthorized disclosure. The changed text modifies this to "advise the Owner within 30 days". This creates a clear contradiction with the Contradicted Text section, specifically part C, which also states the Recipient shall "promptly advise" the owner. Therefore, giving the Recipient 30 days to report a breach directly conflicts with the original immediate requirement, creating an in-text contradiction as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will not copy or modify any Confidential Information without the prior written consent of the Owner.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient may create copies of the Confidential Information for internal archiving purposes, but will not modify it without the prior written consent of the Owner.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by allowing the Recipient to make copies of the Confidential Information for internal archiving, while the original text strictly prohibits any copying without the Owner's consent. This contradiction is contained within Section II ('PROTECTION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION').
perturbation.contradicted_text: PROTECTION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORM ATION. The Recipient understands and acknowledges that the Confidential Information has been developed or obtained by the Owner by the investment of significant time, effort and expense, and that the Confidential Information is a valuable, special and unique asset of the Owner which provides the Owner with a significant competitive advantage, and needs to be protected from improper disclosure. In consideration for the receipt by the Recipient of the Confidential Information, the Recipient agrees as follows: A. No Disclosure. The Recipient will hold the Confidential Information in confidence and will not disclose the Confidential Information to any person or entity without the prior written consent of the Owner. B. No Copying/Modifying. The Recipient will not copy or modify any Confidential Information without the prior written consent of the Owner.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'The Recipient will not copy or modify any Confidential Information without the prior written consent of the Owner'. The changed text states 'The Recipient may create copies of the Confidential Information for internal archiving purposes, but will not modify it without the prior written consent of the Owner.' The changed text introduces a specific allowance for copying that directly contradicts the original blanket prohibition in the 'No Copying/Modifying' section of the contradicted text (PROTECTION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. B. No Copying/Modifying. The Recipient will not copy or modify any Confidential Information without the prior written consent of the Owner.). This is an in-text contradiction as it allows copying in one place while prohibiting it in another, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: During the term of this Agreement and for 3 Years thereafter, Recipient will not attempt to do business with, or otherwise solicit any business contacts found or otherwise referred by Owner to Recipient for the purpose of circumventing, the result of whic h shall be to prevent the Owner from realizing or recognizing a profit, fees, or otherwise, without the specific written approval of the Owner.
perturbation.changed_text: During the term of this Agreement and for 1 Year thereafter, Recipient will not attempt to do business with, or otherwise solicit any business contacts found or otherwise referred by Owner to Recipient for the purpose of circumventing, the result of whic h shall be to prevent the Owner from realizing or recognizing a profit, fees, or otherwise, without the specific written approval of the Owner.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the non-circumvention period from '3 Years thereafter' to '1 Year thereafter' creates a contradiction. This modifies the original period for which the recipient is restricted from engaging in business that circumvents the owner's profits, while section IV originally provided a period of 3 years after the agreement's term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON -CIRCUMVENTION. During the term of this Agreement and for 3 Years thereafter, Recipient will not attempt to do business with, or otherwise solicit any business contacts found or otherwise referred by Owner to Recipient for the purpose of circumventing, the result of whic h shall be to prevent the Owner from realizing or recognizing a profit, fees, or otherwise, without the specific written approval of the Owner. If such circumvention shall occur the Owner shall be entitled to any commissions due pursuant to this Agreement or relating to such transaction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the non-circumvention period from '3 Years thereafter' to '1 Year thereafter'. The contradicted text explicitly states '3 Years thereafter'. This creates a direct and clear inconsistency regarding the duration of the non-circumvention obligation, leading to an in-text contradiction. This falls under the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.
